steps.title: Identify the logical relationship between sentences
steps.content: The first sentence makes a general claim: Spartans were known for laconic wit and maintained it even in peril. The second sentence introduces a specific historical incident. The transition must signal that what follows is a concrete example supporting the claim in the first sentence.

steps.title: Match the transition type to the choices
steps.content:
”For instance” directly introduces an example to illustrate a preceding point—exactly what this passage does: it states a quality Spartans possessed, then demonstrates it with the Philip II anecdote.

The other three signal different relationships: Rather suggests correction or alternative; In other words signals rephrasing; That said introduces a contrast.

None fit moving from a claim to its supporting example. That matches choice D — D is correct.
